islands.name: Future Shores
islands.description: An unfinished island at the edge of Capital — for the kids of the future to carve out their piece and build the games of their era.

learning_objectives: Understand that game worlds are built by people — including you
Experiment with level design and sharing creations
See how financial literacy can be taught through any game style
provenance.originality_notes: Homage to unfinished zones in classic island games — scaffolding left on purpose so the next generation finishes it.

areas.name: The Scaffold
areas.description: Cranes, dashed outlines, and caution tape. This corner of the island is deliberately unfinished — waiting for your ideas.

areas.name: Blank Canvas Bay
areas.description: Empty lots marked with chalk grids. Each plot is a game someone hasn't dreamed up yet. Maybe that someone is you.

areas.name: Community Dock
areas.description: Where creators moor their prototypes. Open VibeCode Studio from home base to publish levels that can live here someday.

items.name: Builder's Chalk
items.description: Mark your territory on the blank canvas.

quests.title: Claim Your Plot
quests.description: The kids of the future get to finish what we started. Explore Future Shores and pick up builder's chalk.

nodes.speaker: The Keeper
nodes.text: Welcome to Future Shores. We left this island unfinished on purpose. The Flash kids had their sites. The Poptropica kids had their islands. Now it's your turn — carve out games for your era in VibeCode Studio.

choices.text: So I get to build what comes next?
What happened to the old islands?

nodes.speaker: The Keeper
nodes.text: Exactly. Sail to the other islands to see how each era looked — Poptropica sketch, Flash flat, Miniclip glossy. Then come back here and make something nobody's seen before.

nodes.speaker: The Keeper
nodes.text: They're still out there — each island keeps its own animation style. Your character morphs when you land. But this shore? It's all scaffolding until you and your friends fill it in.
